raw_input:
[5.0/5] Super Easy To Apply, Does A Great Job Smoothing My Fine Flyaway Hairs & Haven’t Had Any Issues I have fine hair that I blow dry to add style and smooth frizz. I can usually get the top pretty smooth but have some baby hairs and bad hair days where little hairs stick out and adding more of my regular lightweight gel or other products can weigh my hair down too much, make it look wet or dirty or make it clumpy or stiff. I don’t know why but I didn’t have high hopes that this Wing Fly Grl Mane Taming Wand gel would be anything special, but it really works great for my fine hair.

It’s about the size of a mascara and has a wand with a small foam type applicator (similar to one on a lip gloss) at the end. I just gently run it over the hairs sticking up on top and they just lay down and blend right in with the rest of my hair. I can’t say how this works for thick hair, but it sure is fast and easy to use and works great for me. My hair is very dark brown and I can’t see any sign of the gel, just the benefit of smooth hair.

I haven’t experienced any flaking, dryness, stiffness, wet look, burning or discomfort or issues with my hair color. This wand is convenient as it’s so compact and easy to carry in my purse on-the-go. The ingredient list is short and no ingredients stand out as worrisome health risks or as being possibly damaging to my hair. It works like a charm smoothing my little flyaways and so far has no negative consequences. I’ll update if I see any issues in the longer term, but it’s all good so far. Love it. 5 stars.

Ingredients: Purified Water, Glycerin 99.% Usp Kosher, Polyacrylate Crosspolymer-6, Phenoxyethanol, Caprylyl Glycol, Ethylhexlglycerin, Provitamin B5 (D-panthenol) - Powder. [5.0/5] Loving My Hair!! This succulent gel is my ‘saving grace’ when my locks of hair need that finishing touch of perfection! It is fantastic at pampering my hair with a subduing presence that sleeks down my unruly strains, trying to undo my groomed beauty. Just a thin layer applied to the top of my hair, graces my curls with a silky, nurturing caress - this gel works wonders! [5.0/5] Works surprisingly well!! I was optimistically skeptical about this product, but it works just as described. It does tame (kinda locks down) those fly away pieces. Much more targeted, quicker, and requires less product application than hairspray. Doesn't leave hair hard to touch or greasy. I did have to dip the applicator into the bottle before each little section of hair and am guessing I'll go through a bottle fairly quickly. Glad I gave it a try. Will use it on important hair days for sure. [5.0/5] Does its job well A fantastic cruelty free item to have in your purse. Takes away any flyaways that are driving you crazy! During in the summer I tend to wear my hair pulled back in a ponytail, and always find that I have those stray flyaway hairs. This easily and quickly takes care of them. Just pull out the wand, tame them down using the tip of the wand, and go about your business. Holds for the better part of the day. You will need to reapply every now and then, but it doesn’t leave your hair feeling oily or weighed down. Definitely some thing I’ll continue to use. [4.0/5] Good hold I have tried hair flyaway gel before that comes with a mascara wand but this one comes with a wand that looks like it would go to a lipgloss. I feel like the wand itself makes it messier to apply because you are just rubbing it on your hair instead of brushing it to catch all the flyaways. The gel itself works well but you just have to apply a little more pressure with the wand to get it to go where you want to go. [4.0/5] Small Wand Maybe my flyaways are just bigger than the average person's, but I thought the wand was a little small. It's essentially the size of a lip gloss or mascara. I feel like the wand tip would work better if it had something like a mascara wand tip so it helps comb the wispies back. The stuff itself works well without leaving your hair looking or feeling oily. The application was a bit tedious and time consuming for me personally. [4.0/5] Works well I was surprised to see that the applicator is a lambs ear type brush versus one with bristles, but it does the job! It smoothed my fly always down and once dry (it goes on wet), everything stayed in place despite some light winds. It won’t work in heavy wind, but it provides a light hold that isn’t crunchy.

It won’t hold big pieces, and it doesn’t last all day. But having said that, it lasts a long time in normal circumstances. Keep it in your purse for touch ups. [4.0/5] It is okay It doesn't keep my baby hairs down for too long. I wish it had a stronger hold [3.0/5] Didn’t work too well This didn’t do much. I still had to use a little bit of hairspray to tame my flyaways. It did work but not the whole way. [3.0/5] Why is this so expensive? Ok so this entire tube fits in the palm of my hand. It’s like the size of a lip gloss or mascara. So, with that being said why is it so expensive???! I have a hard time getting past that fact. I thought oh boy this better be good for the price.

So this is pretty much identical in both applicator and size to a lip gloss. I like the idea of the applicator being mess free something you can carry in your pocket and do a quick touch up on the go without putting product on your hands. The gel itself isn’t sticky and doesn’t dry with any “crunch” to it. Which is both good and bad. It’s not overly heavy for quick touch ups. But for someone like me with really curly frizzy hair this provides no lasting power. You can hardly tell I used it a short while after application.

I’d say all in all you can buy a whole bottle of lightweight hair gel (basically all this is) for about 1/10 of the price. I wouldn’t purchase this in the future. [4.0/5] Decent Product My daughter has fine hair and always has flyaways. This is helpful but I wish it had a comb like applicator rather than one that looks like lipgloss. She has sensitive skin and this didn't cause her skin to react to it when applied. [2.0/5] Why this tip! I use this kind of stuff all the time for my girls. My 9 yr old is obsessed with the brush kind. This wand is ridiculous! I absolutely hate how it goes on and it doesn't work anywhere near as well as the brush. Who decided this eye brush would be a good idea?! Stick with the mascara brush! It works perfectly, this doesn't! [5.0/5] It Works! My hair is exceptionally fine and fly-away. So, when I saw this, Fly Grl Mane-Taming Gel Wand by Wing, I had to try it.

When I saw its size, I really thought it would not be enough to tame all my hair. It is merely the size of a mascara. But it did calm those fly-away hairs! It is clear, so it is not visible when it is applied. All I had to do was apply the product with the wand to the fly-aways, and they were instantly under control. Fly Grl also adds shine.

The ingredients are also supposed to condition and strengthen my hair, which is also very welcome. It contains glycerin and panthenol.

This handy product is made in the United States. [4.0/5] Worked well The applicator is the type that you would normally see in a tube of concealer when similar products have a mascara type of spoolie. If you were trying to tame the hair in the picture shown above it would take forever but taming one or two loose hairs when you have it in a ponytail is super quick and easy. The hold lasts pretty much the entire time I have my hair up. As far as value, I think you'd be able to find less expensive options or even have the same results spraying some hairspray on your hand and smoothing it over your hair. [3.0/5] Pro: Doesn't Smell Con: Doesn't Work I have another hair taming gel from some other brand that works REALLY REALLY GOOD but has a horrible smell that I simply can't stand. But this one has ZERO smell and doesn't work AT ALL. [4.0/5] Works on some hair I have some baby hairs, some frizzy hairs and some wiry colored white hair that pokes out of my pony tail and blows up in the wind. This is definitely not for the frizzy or thick hairs. It did basically nothing on those. I used it on my very fine baby hairs and around my face and it smoothed the hairs down well and looked a lot more polished. I could have probably achieved the same result with hairspray. I assume this is for a quick fix and to carry in your purse. It only works on fine hair and not a great fit for me. [3.0/5] Works great! I have fine hair with some thicker, unruly hairs and frizzies that refuse to cooperate on top. This gel definitely did the trick for most of the pieces I put it on but the thicker ones bounce right back up. If you only need to touch up a few hairs here and there, this is a great product for you. If you have quite a few, I would say maybe skip this product as the wand is so small that it would take way too long and isn't realistic to use on a daily basis.
I am confused about the price point; the portability and quality are fantastic but would be more willing to purchase again if the price was lower. [3.0/5] Works Okay. Very Expensive. My daughter has frizzy hair, especially in the summer, and she was really hoping this would help. Well, it did but she'd probably use the whole bottle in a few days.

This bottle is about the size of mascara. It's on the small side. The applicator was nice, it worked well and was easy to use. My daughter has frizzy hair and was really looking forward to this. It does work, but it's such a small bottle!

Overall, it works well but there's cheaper and bigger sized alternatives out there. This is so expensive for what it is, currently $19.99 for a little 8 ml bottle. I just couldn't justify getting this again.

This would be okay for minor touch-ups but if you have frizzy hair, try something else that's cheaper, and bigger. [5.0/5] Fly away no more I was really surprised on how well this worked and a little bit does. Go a long way [5.0/5] No more fly-Aways! If you’re like me, and have ultra fine hair with a ton of fly-away strands around the frame of your face, then this is an absolute must have for you!
I am sooo glad a product like this exists as it’s been a true lifesaver for me!
Very easy to use, just lightly brush the wand over the unwanted fly away strands and lay them back against your hair,and they stay like magic!
I always spray a small amount of hairspray after using the wand, just to help lock it in, and have had no issues with my hair not staying in place.
This stuff is truly awesome and a must have for anyone with unruly fly-away hair!

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

Most customers are happy with this flyaway hair taming gel wand, giving it mostly 4 and 5 star reviews. The product works well for smoothing fine, flyaway hairs and baby hairs without leaving hair feeling oily, stiff, or crunchy. It is easy to use, compact enough to carry in a purse, and works quickly. Many reviewers appreciate that it does not weigh hair down or leave a wet or greasy look. The ingredients are considered safe and even beneficial, containing glycerin and panthenol.

The main complaints are about the small size of the product and its price. At around $19.99 for an 8ml bottle, several reviewers felt it was too expensive for the amount of product you get. Some also felt the wand applicator could be improved, with a few preferring a comb or brush style tip over the current foam or spoolie design. A small number of reviewers found it did not work well enough for thicker or very frizzy hair, and a couple noted it had an unpleasant smell.

Overall, the product is best suited for people with fine hair who need a quick, portable solution for taming flyaways and baby hairs, though some feel there are cheaper alternatives that offer similar results.
